summary.intent: Address two rounds of Codex AI code review findings on PR #687 in the fogodev/ars-ui repository, fixing bugs in the RangeSlider component related to drag behavior, controlled prop syncing, thumb tab sequence, and accessible names.
summary.outcome: Both rounds of Codex review findings were resolved: the first pass fixed blur-cancels-drag and controlled swap drag math bugs (commit 5a41f3b7), and the second pass fixed bounds-before-controlled-value ordering, both-thumbs tabbable, and distinct per-thumb accessible names (a third commit). All targeted tests passed including unit regressions, spec-conformance, and snapshot validation.

repo: The project uses a Codex AI reviewer that signals review state via GitHub reactions: 'eyes' means actively reviewing, '+1' means approval, and new unresolved threads signal new findings.
The waiting-for-codex-review loop is a formalized skill at .agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md with a specific polling protocol.
Snapshots are managed with cargo-insta; intentional behavior changes require `cargo insta accept` before the full suite passes.

The RangeSlider component is in crates/ars-components/src/input/range_slider/mod.rs and has both unit tests and spec-conformance tests (cargo test --test spec_conformance range_slider_anatomy_matches_spec).
Coverage is measured with cargo-llvm-cov and targeted per-module runs are supported.
Commits require a Codex co-author line when fixes address Codex review findings.
The `--unreferenced=reject` flag on `cargo insta test` scans all crate snapshots regardless of filter, causing false positives from unrelated fixtures when used to gate a focused module.

code.finding: on_props_changed / SetProps handler was applying controlled value sync before updating bounds, causing stale clamp behavior (e.g., value clamped to [100,100] with new wider bounds).
The unfocused thumb was rendered with tabindex=-1, removing it from the tab sequence. Both enabled thumbs should always be reachable via Tab.

A shared aria-label on the component was overriding per-thumb aria-labelledby, collapsing both thumb accessible names to the same string. Per-thumb labels (start_label/end_label) must take precedence.

workflow: Thread-aware Codex review polling requires querying GitHub GraphQL for unresolved threads authored by 'codex' rather than relying on reaction states alone.
The TDD flow here was strict: add failing regression tests first, confirm they fail, then apply implementation changes, confirm they pass, then commit.
cargo +nightly fmt --all --check should be run before final commit validation; formatting failures will block the CI chain.
When polling an AI reviewer with sleep loops, absence of new threads during 'eyes' reaction does not mean approval — continued polling is required until the reaction changes.

friction: cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject ignores --glob-filter and scans the entire crate, reporting unrelated unreferenced snapshots as failures even when the target module's snapshots are all valid.
Multiple cargo insta accept cycles were needed because snapshot diffs emerged iteratively as implementation patches landed.
The Codex review second pass took multiple polling cycles (7+ at 120s each) before threads appeared, requiring patience in the loop.
GraphQL thread IDs were redacted in the transcript, requiring careful handling to avoid exposing sensitive identifiers.
open_items: The --unreferenced=reject snapshot gate does not scope correctly to a single module; a project-level solution (e.g., per-module snapshot directories or a filtered insta configuration) should be explored to make the gate reliable.
CI checks for coverage, Dioxus/Leptos feature flags, and release verification were still pending at the time of the second commit — their final status was not confirmed in the transcript.
The Codex review reaction (+1 approval) was not confirmed at session end; the PR may still be awaiting final Codex sign-off.
